_description: Model derivations for Argentina vs Cape Verde 2026-07-03 R32

elo.flag: Agrees directionally with market (85.5%) -- both sources confirm this is the most lopsided favorite of the campaign so far.

rule_checks.RULE7: FIRES HARD -- Cape Verde win=3.51%, the most extreme underdog price seen this campaign (more extreme than SUI-ALG 21%, ESP-AUT 7.3%, ENG-CDR 5.6%). Edge focus shifts to Cape Verde-side props.
rule_checks.RULE15: FIRES Scenario A for Q6 (Cape Verde 2+ SOT) -- CPV win probability (3.5%) is far below the 10% blowout threshold. Most-analogous precedent (CPV vs Spain, comparable elite-press opponent quality) produced just 1 SOT/6 shots/25.7% possession. Market's 51.36% raw price is shaded down materially.
rule_checks.RULE12: Flagged for Q4 (Lautaro) and Q10 (Alvarez) -- both players have very thin/poor group-stage production (Lautaro: 1 goal in 3, a penalty, 0 open-play; Alvarez: 0 goals, 0 assists, benched twice). Market prices trusted but trimmed modestly for this signal.

espn_insight.messi_group_form: 6 goals in 3 group games (hat-trick, brace, 1 off the bench) -- exceptional current form, SOT 4/4/1 (avg 3.0)
espn_insight.lautaro_group_form: 1 goal in 3 (penalty only, 0 open play), SOT 1/0/1 -- clearly the tournament's quiet man among ARG's front three so far
espn_insight.alvarez_group_form: 0G/0A in 3 games, started only 1 of 3 -- rotation/impact role, not a nailed-on starter
espn_insight.cape_verde_vs_elite_press: Their Spain game (74% opponent possession, 27 shots against) is the single most relevant comparable for today's likely game state -- 1 SOT, 6 shots, 1 foul committed all match (extremely passive low block)
